title: Attached
author: Amir Levine & Rachel Heller
subline: The new science of adult attachment.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- Two silhouettes facing each other across a gap. Each silhouette has a small symbol near the chest representing their attachment style. The line between them is a long red 'secure base' line. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1100)" fill="none">
    <!-- Left silhouette: an anxious attacher (taller, leaning) -->
    <g stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2">
      <circle cx="-280" cy="-300" r="40"/>
      <line x1="-280" y1="-260" x2="-280" y2="0"/>
      <line x1="-280" y1="-200" x2="-340" y2="-100"/>
      <line x1="-280" y1="-200" x2="-220" y2="-100"/>
      <line x1="-280" y1="0" x2="-320" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="-280" y1="0" x2="-240" y2="120"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Right silhouette: an avoidant attacher (arms crossed) -->
    <g stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2">
      <circle cx="280" cy="-300" r="40"/>
      <line x1="280" y1="-260" x2="280" y2="0"/>
      <line x1="280" y1="-200" x2="240" y2="-100"/>
      <line x1="280" y1="-200" x2="320" y2="-100"/>
      <line x1="280" y1="-200" x2="240" y2="-160"/>
      <line x1="280" y1="-200" x2="320" y2="-160"/>
      <line x1="280" y1="0" x2="240" y2="120"/>
      <line x1="280" y1="0" x2="320" y2="120"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The 'secure base' line: a single red horizontal line at foot level connecting them -->
    <line x1="-280" y1="120" x2="280" y2="120" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- 'A' label on left -->
    <text x="-280" y="-360" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="32" font-weight="300" fill="#ffffff" letter-spacing="0.04em">A</text>
    <!-- 'B' label on right -->
    <text x="280" y="-360" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="32" font-weight="300" fill="#ffffff" letter-spacing="0.04em">B</text>
    <!-- '?' between them -->
    <text x="0" y="0" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="80" font-weight="300" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.04em">?</text>
  </g>
  <text x="800" y="320" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">FIG. 1 · THE ATTACHMENT GAP</text>
</svg>
rationale: Two stick-figure silhouettes face each other across an empty middle: an anxious attacher (taller, leaning in) on the left, an avoidant attacher (arms crossed) on the right. A red horizontal line at their feet is the 'secure base' — the only chromatic element. The 560px 'ATTACHED' title in weight 500 sits above. The visual thesis: attachment is the dance between two styles, mediated by the secure base.
